verdict.headline: Do NOT transact — wallet is on OFAC SDN and linked to the DPRK Lazarus Group.
verdict.reasoning: The sanctions screen returned a confirmed match against three programs (OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, DPRK) with a high risk level, which is a hard veto under our policy and overrides any other signal. On-chain history shows a funded wallet (~101.8 ETH) but this does not mitigate sanctions exposure — sending funds would likely violate U.S. sanctions law. Labels and web_sentiment endpoints were enumerated but returned no exonerating evidence. Confidence is high based solely on the sanctions hit.

findings.finding: Confirmed sanctions match on OFAC SDN, Lazarus Group, and DPRK programs (risk_level: high). Hard veto.
Label provider responded but returned no specific labels for this address; no exonerating evidence.
Wallet holds ~101.80 ETH; balance and activity do not offset sanctions exposure.
Reputation API metadata returned but no concrete sentiment payload; not exonerating.

services.rationale: Resource 0 is the only sanctions candidate with a confirmed 0% recent failure rate AND inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, making it the most reliable pick; its $0.001 price is also the lowest available.

Resource 1 is the only labels candidate with a confirmed 0% recent failure rate, making it the standout choice despite all candidates sharing the same 1/3 inputInfoCompleteness; its $0.005 price is also competitive.
Resource 1 is the only onchain_history candidate with both a confirmed 0% recent failure rate and a higher inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, clearly dominating the field.

Resource 2 has a confirmed 0% recent failure rate (ruling out resource 1 at 100% failure); among the two 0%-failure candidates, resource 2's description of a 0–100 risk/reputation score more directly matches web-sentiment intent, breaking the tie over resource 0.
